behaviors.description: Error message names missing required key by exact config path (e.g., 'database.host')
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the validation logic itself—given a config structure missing a required key, the code must report the exact path. This is pure logic with no I/O dependency. A unit test loads a config dict (no file), triggers validation, and asserts the error message contains the correct path. No mock can hide this because the assertion directly checks the error output.
behaviors.test_description: Load a config dict with a missing nested required key (e.g., absent 'database.host'), trigger validation, assert the error message names the exact path 'database.host'.

behaviors.description: When XDG_CONFIG_HOME is set, loader resolves config path relative to that directory, not ~/.config
behaviors.justification: The real failure is: code reads from ~/.config when it should use XDG_CONFIG_HOME. Unit test with mocked I/O catches this by asserting the exact path passed to the file-open call. If code ignores the env var, the assertion on the path argument fails—the mock doesn't hide the bug because we verify which path gets used, not whether the mock succeeds. No filesystem I/O needed to verify path construction logic.
behaviors.test_description: Set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to a temp path (e.g., /tmp/test), mock the file-open call, invoke load_config(), assert that open() was called with the path under /tmp/test, not ~/.config.

behaviors.description: Environment variable with TOOL_ prefix overrides config file value
behaviors.justification: The failure is in the merge logic: given config values and env vars, env vars must take precedence. This is pure dictionary/object merging logic with no I/O or external dependencies. Unit test loads a config dict, sets an env var, and asserts the merged result uses the env var value. No file operations or mocking of I/O boundaries needed.
behaviors.test_description: Load config with a key (e.g., 'database.host=filehost'), set TOOL_DATABASE_HOST=envhost, invoke merge logic, assert the final config uses 'envhost'.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax error in config file includes line number in error message
behaviors.justification: The failure is: error reporting logic doesn't extract or include the line number from the YAML parser. This can be tested with a YAML string (no file needed)—pass invalid YAML directly to the parser, catch the exception, and assert the error message includes the line number. The parsing library provides line numbers in exceptions; the code just needs to report them. No filesystem I/O required.
behaviors.test_description: Parse a YAML string with a syntax error (e.g., mismatched indentation on line 5), catch the parse exception, assert the error message includes line number 5.

behaviors.description: When config directory does not exist at startup, loader creates it and writes a defaults file
behaviors.justification: MOCK-MISS: A Unit test with mocked os.makedirs() and open() will always pass, hiding real failures. In production, the real failure is: code fails to actually create the directory or write the file (typo in path, missing os.makedirs call, permission denied, etc.). Mocking these operations masks the failure. Integration test with real temp filesystem is the minimum: delete the config directory, call load_config(), verify the directory and file were actually created with correct content. This catches real file operation failures.
behaviors.test_description: Use a temporary directory as home, ensure config directory does not exist, invoke load_config(), assert config directory was created and defaults file exists with valid YAML content.
